serving.natural_language_portion: One black sesame ball from Old Chang Kee weighs approximately 35 g and is typically consumed as a single-piece snack or dessert.

nutrient_source.title: Tang Yuan, Black Sesame Filling — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA SR Legacy analogous estimate for glutinous rice ball with sesame filling
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo data for tang yuan (glutinous rice ball with sweet filling) and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy entries for glutinous rice flour (USDA FDC ID 169715) and black sesame seeds (USDA FDC ID 169414). The composite reflects approximately 70% glutinous rice flour shell and 30% black sesame-sugar-fat filling, consistent with standard tangyuan formulations. Values are estimated where exact HPB Foodo data for black sesame tangyuan are not individually listed.

gi_source.title: Glutinous rice-based confection GI — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entry for glutinous rice products
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for black sesame tangyuan. GI of 61 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for glutinous rice-based foods (GI range 60–65 reported for boiled glutinous rice products). The fat and fibre content of the sesame filling moderates the overall GI slightly below plain glutinous rice. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (61 × 15.6) / 100 ≈ 9.5, where carbohydrate per 35 g serving = 44.5 × 0.35 = 15.6 g.

hypertension.guidance: Black sesame ball contains approximately 28 mg sodium per 100 g, making it a low-sodium snack that poses minimal direct risk for hypertension when consumed in moderation as a single-piece serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index and refined carbohydrate content from glutinous rice flour
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 61 and a glycemic load of ~9.5 per 35 g serving, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption to one piece per occasion and pair with a protein or fibre source to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat content with favourable unsaturated-to-saturated fat ratio and negligible cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Black sesame ball provides approximately 9.8 g fat per 100 g, predominantly unsaturated fats from sesame seeds, with low saturated fat (2.1 g) and negligible cholesterol, making it acceptable for cardiovascular health when consumed occasionally as a small snack.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Black Sesame Ball (黑芝麻汤圆, Hēi Zhīma Tāngyuán) is a traditional Chinese glutinous rice dumpling filled with a smooth, sweet black sesame paste. The outer shell is made from glutinous rice flour mixed with water and shaped into a round ball, while the filling consists of ground black sesame seeds blended with sugar and a small amount of fat (traditionally lard, though vegetable shortening is common in modern commercial versions). The balls are boiled until they float and become soft and chewy. In Singapore, they are sold as a popular snack and dessert item, notably by Old Chang Kee. The texture is characteristically soft, sticky, and slightly chewy on the outside, with a rich, nutty, and mildly sweet sesame filling inside. Black sesame is nutritionally notable for its calcium, iron, magnesium, and phosphorus content, as well as its polyunsaturated fatty acids. The dish carries cultural significance in Chinese tradition, particularly during the Lantern Festival (Yuan Xiao) and Winter Solstice (Dong Zhi). Clinically, the medium glycemic index (~61) and moderate carbohydrate content (44.5 g per 100 g) mean that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this food in moderation. The low sodium content makes it suitable for those managing hypertension. The favourable unsaturated fat profile from sesame seeds supports cardiovascular health when consumed as an occasional snack.

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo entry for black sesame tangyuan was individually retrievable; nutrient values are adapted from HPB Foodo tang yuan category data and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy entries for glutinous rice flour and black sesame seeds using a standard 70:30 shell-to-filling ratio. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for glutinous rice-based products. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates based on HPB Singapore Foodo tang yuan category and USDA SR Legacy for glutinous rice flour (FDC ID 169715) and black sesame seeds (FDC ID 169414), using an approximate 70% shell / 30% filling ratio. Direct laboratory analysis of Old Chang Kee's specific product was not available. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — glutinous rice flour and sesame seeds contain no industrially produced trans fats; confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy). 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant-based ingredients contain no cholesterol; lard variant would introduce cholesterol but commercial versions typically use vegetable shortening). 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no vitamin C-containing ingredients in standard formulation). 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no animal-derived ingredients in standard commercial vegetarian version). 6. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no vitamin D source in this food). 7. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 8. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 9. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 10. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 11. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 12. GI of 61 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database glutinous rice product entries; no direct measurement for black sesame tangyuan was found. 13. The cuisine tag in the input JSON was listed as 'Curry Puff', which appears to be a data entry error from the field survey seed record; this record has been correctly classified as Chinese cuisine / glutinous rice snack. 14. is_vegan set to false as traditional formulation uses lard; commercial versions may vary. 15. FoodOn URI FOODON_03302050 and FoodEx2 code A0CJW are used as closest parent matches; no exact ontology codes exist for black sesame tangyuan specifically.
